source_fact_summary: The public issue says a code agent added a column-alignment test with two session counts that both rendered as the same-length value. Because the padding operation was a no-op for those values, the test could pass even if the alignment logic was removed; a later fix used asymmetric values.
category_change_reason: No category change suggested: the source describes a test whose chosen values failed to exercise the alignment behavior under test.
privacy_findings: Only public GitHub issue or PR links and short factual summaries are retained; no credentials, private files, raw logs, or local absolute paths were copied.
severity_reason: The failure weakens UI-formatting coverage but concerns a narrow display alignment behavior.
missing_information: Manual review should inspect the exact test values and final corrected test before formalization.
scope_reason: The issue is directly tied to Agent-authored tests in a project artifact. A mutation check that removes padding, combined with the equal-value test data, can independently show the test was not detecting the target behavior.
notes: Codex review suggestion only: public-source accessibility and category/scope recommendations do not mark the candidate source_verified, approved, or reproduced. Manual confirmation remains required.
